meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold rush context, Wintu presence, mining communities, and rugged regional work.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Wintu homeland around Clear Creek and the need to show Native presence as grounded community context.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining town material: brick remnants, commerce, courthouse memory, iron, timber, and road dust.

image_prompt.subject: a full-packet hero plate of French Gulch in 1899: muddy main street, timber storefronts, ore wagons, pine hills, and one rider entering from the near foreground
image_prompt.scene: late afternoon after rain; chimney smoke, slick ruts, tethered horses, a courthouse notice board with unreadable papers, a Wintu family and a freight hand crossing paths naturally near the store, and mountains closing the sky
image_prompt.composition: square promotional key art crop with the road pulling the eye into town; side characters arranged like a playable hub, with no single posed hero owning the frame
image_prompt.camera: low horseback-height three-quarter view, mild wide angle, early-HD open-world game framing with readable silhouettes and chunky environmental geometry
material_cues: wet mud ruts reflecting gray sky
dark pine boards swollen by rain
lamp glass catching dull brass light
iron wagon rims and ore cart scars
worn saddle leather and soaked wool
tailings and pine needles at the road edge
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make this the visual promise of the whole packet: not clean nostalgia, but a town where every porch has a private argument and every road out costs more than it first looks.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD promotional render mixed with in-engine screenshot staging
render_traits: slightly simplified geometry with readable silhouettes
modest texture resolution and baked sunlight
hard-edged ambient occlusion under awnings, hats, wagons, and porch rails
believable 1899 clothing, tools, saddles, firearms, wagons, animals, and town architecture
dust, mud, smoke, pine needles, canvas, iron, rawhide, calico, paper, and worn paint as tactile cues
style_modifiers.palette: sun-baked ochre, pine green, muddy umber, charcoal shadow, oxidized iron, faded calico, dull brass, cold creek blue, and smoke gray; avoid one-note sepia.

text_rendering.instructions: Avoid readable words. Notices, signs, and paper scraps may appear only as indistinct period marks.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The main subject is fully framed and not hidden by text or logos.
Every visible prop, animal, garment, and building belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier life.
Native presence is ordinary, respectful, and grounded in community life rather than costume or spectacle.
No copied game characters, no readable modern text, and no inherited non-western symbols.
